channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: My coworker used my personal email to track down my twitter and sent it to HR. — 8 replies — 32.0/hr

My coworker used my personal email to track down my twitter and sent it to HR.

Open thread • Replies: 8 • Updated: 2026-03-30 06:58 UTC

OP

My coworker used my personal email to track down my twitter and sent it to HR. Wtf. Im getting called in for supporting Hezbollah

62051010
>>62051008
When did people start connecting their real identity to online accounts? I remember when you never said your real name online ever.

62051045
>>62051008
easy suit

62051096
>>62051045
I got a warning

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62051099
>>62051010

62051100
>>62051096
you got evidence, keep stacking

62051104
>>62051010
>>62051099
you use clearnets in the big 26?

62051109
>>62051104
Dont remind me

62051110
>>62051010

62033417
everybody hates u stupid stinks
we loathe you more than almost anything
fuck every single mother fuckin stink
you're all the worst we hate you so dang much

item.title: US here. Should I buy a house (cash) before hyperinflation kicks in ? — 14 replies — 22.1/hr

US here. Should I buy a house (cash) before hyperinflation kicks in ?

Open thread • Replies: 14 • Updated: 2026-03-30 06:21 UTC

OP

Utilities will probably be crazy.

Early replies (first 3)

62050666
never buy boomer bags

62050681
>>62050666
You objectively cannot argue with these digits OP.

62050682
>>62050666
what do then

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62050687
>>62050681
god damn
>>62050666
ccccc-c-c-checked

62050688
>>62050681
>trusting satan

62050749
>>62050666
FPBP
>Satanic trips don't lie

62050908
>>62050682
Hyperinflation isn't coming, economic collapse and apocalyptic deflation is

62050911
>>62050908
Nah, i think stagflation is more likely.

62050912
>>62050649
yeah dude this is the best time in history to buy a house

62050914
>>62050688
Checked.
>>62050681
He's not called the father of lies for nothing, anon.

62050944
>>62050912
In ALL of history.
>>62050649
I think like 2028-2030.

62050960
>>62050666
holy BAAAAAAAAAAASED

62051016
>>62050911
isn't it "very high" inflation instead ? both are suboptimal

item.title: Polkadot - First halving — 208 replies — 27.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
Polkadot - First halving

Open thread • Replies: 208 • Updated: 2026-03-29 23:24 UTC

OP

https://polkadothalving.com/ 10 more days. https://www.parity.io/blog/refining-polkadots-economic-architecture-issuance-DOT-DAP-and-network-adjustments
https://x.com/Polkadot/status/2028482336463945765
"
Polkadot’s economic upgrade begins rolling out in 10 days.

Enhanced tokenomics increases DOT scarcity and introduces new governance and staking mechanisms.

▸ DOT supply capped at 2.1B
▸ Emissions cut 53.6%
▸ Unbonding from 28 days to 24-48 hours
"

Early replies (first 3)

61933757
Guys?

61933779
>>61932834
10000$ @ 6.5
i dont sell my dotty

61936705
Guys?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62037187
>>62034101
>178 replies
>105 pbtid

oh I dunno

62037296
>>62037187
Gavin is paying me to keep the thread alive. This is how we live in Mumbai my good sir. We shill coins while talking about the mind.

62037323
>>62037187
I need rupees to worship the Vishnu on the blockchain my good sar. And to feed my village of Mumbai, the city of DOT.

62037509
>>62037296
>>62037323

Post hand

62039073
>>62037509
I already did here >>62008295. True Mumbai warrior hand. My fathers name was Kumar, my mothers name was Lakshmi. Pic related. I'm the little prince

62039085
>>62037509
I am now a Vishnu warrior of the blockchain, living in the city of DOT, Mumbai. I run a scam call center, they call me papa Rupee over here. I make many Rupees for many villages. We feed whole of city of DOT, Mumbai. We eat very good. Our bellies are very full.

62040207
>>62039073
>>62039085

Shitposting or on hard drugs constantly?

62040932
>>62040207
I don't get the question? Help me understand what you mean. And thanks for bumping the thread. Gavin Wood, our lord and saviour, will be very pleased with you.

May the blockchain Vishnu be with you good sar

62041893
>>62040932
Read again slowly

62042472
>>62041893
Yea..... You're asking me if I'm shitposting or on hard drugs constantly? That's your question? I can't decipher your half-formed sentences to be honest.

62043369
>>62042472
Simple yes or no

62044274
>>62043369
I can't answer if you can't formulate and define what the question is, right? So, please define the question and then I can answer it

62044411
i'm very sad for this investment

62044561
>>62044411
That sounds really tough my fren. Siddartha Gautama said "Attachment is the root cause of all suffering.". Just practice detachment and sooner or later you'll be able to accept your loss. Maybe even grief it if it's a big loss

62044605
>>62044411
May the Vishnu on the blockchain be with you

62044783
>>62044411
Another thing Siddartha Gautama said is that the only two things you're entitled two in life is suffering and death.

62045355
>>62044561
>>62044605
>>62044783

Not very encouraging to attract new buyers

62045388
>>62045355
Ah. How so?

62045442
>>62045388
Perhaps share influential news

We know DOT is a game changer in the Web world.

62045475
>>62045442
>Perhaps share influential news
Like what?

62045958
>>62045442
100$ end of bull run. Here are some influential news for you

62048109
May Vishnu of the blockchain bless you and bless DOT to 100$.

Best regard from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar

62048431
At least 119 of 200 posts are by OP. This should violate a rule.

62048708
>>62045475
You mean there are none?
>>62048431
Obamaleaf was worse

62048850
>>62048431
>You mean there are none?
What would you regard as "influential news"?

62048939
>>62048850
What do you think? Can you define influential

62048959
>>62048939
>Can you define influential
No, because it's a subjective word. To some people a towns hall meting of a town of 500 people might be the most "influential" thing in the world since it affect them, their home town and their lives. To someone else it's a completely irrelevant event because it doesn't affect anyone or anything in their own lives. To some people the war in Iran is completely unimportant and doesn't affect them. To some people, like the Iranians is a life changing event.

The word "influential" news is in the perception of the beholder. I can give you as much stuff as you want, it's just... If you don't care about it then it's not "influental", that's why I'm asking
>What would you regard as "influential news"?
Since every individual themselves need to determine what THEY think is influential for them and tickles their funny bone.

62049007
>>62048939
Here, a google definiton; Influential describes
someone or something with the power to cause change, shape decisions, or exert significant impact on people or events. It implies having high status, authority, or being highly important (e.g., an influential leader or book).

62049042
>>62048939
>>62045442
>>62048708
If you can't even define what you want then how can I even give it to you, right? I can only give you something you can actually define.

62049654
>>62048959
>>62049007
>>62049042

You literally shill this fucking coin. Do some influencing

item.title: What the fuck is going on with MSFT? How is this not a great entry? Convince me to not go all in by monday. — 53 replies — 26.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
What the fuck is going on with MSFT? How is this not a great entry? Convince me to not go all in by monday.

Open thread • Replies: 53 • Updated: 2026-03-30 04:58 UTC

OP

What the fuck is going on with MSFT?
How is this not a great entry?
Convince me to not go all in by monday.

Early replies (first 3)

62043952
because the music has stopped

fucking cretin

62043962
Just buy gold and gold mining stocks.

62043971
>>62043947
I would wait until i see signs of of absolute panic.
And even then only go in for a medium term trade.
Liquidity is not great and there are big IPOs coming that will absorb even more of it.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62048812
Main product? AI and jeetcode shit
Side products? Expensive jeetcode
Business products? Subpar and their de facto hold is crumbling rapidly.

I'd stay clear of MSFT like an STD ridden rabid animal.

62048818
was gonna mass reply to everyone bullish on jeetsoft but it was only op
and everyone else has already said what i was gonna say
love you guys

62048822
more like macroshart

62048912
please don't talk about anything other than chainlink

62049005
looks like it's forming head and shoulders tho

62049023
>>62043947
I refuse to buy a company that only produces spyware

62049404
>>62043947
Because they're going to continue getting rid of or enshitifying all of their revenue streams that actually matter so they can pursue ones that currently don't matter but may in the future. The beatings will continue until morale improves basically.

62049436
>>62043947
Xbox is 100% dead, they spent 15 years making every bad decision they possibly could until the last person decided to avoid Xbox.
Apple Macbook NEO will totally take over the mid-range 400-1000 dollar laptop market. NEO is superior than any Windows PC until you get into the 1,500 dollar+ range so it's a no-brainer.
Google Sheets/Docs is superior than MS word/sheets

The only thing Microjeet has left are boomer corps but they will eventually switch to apple/mac products since it is superior in every single way.
I have been using Microsoft products for 30 years so I know how to fix every jeet-coded bullshit that never ends, but I would NEVER EVER in a million years recommend a windows PC to anyone

62049474
>>62043976
Windows has been semi-free for a retail customer for over a decade, since Windows 10 released.

62049557
>>62049436
>>62049404
>>62048780
Y'all being edgy and toxic over the company you hate, that's palpable, but the truth is it's just a correction after a steep ascent. There is nothing wrong with Microsoft.

62049571
>>62049557
>There is nothing wrong with Microsoft

That is simply wrong, what happened at Xbox division the last 15 years will be studied in future finance major classes until the end of time. It was a total self-destruction caused by misunderstanding their own product and fan-base.
If I was in charge of Xbox in 2010 they would be the #1 console on Earth, I would have fully pivoted to Xbox Arcade as the center-piece of the console and focused on dude-bro games like Gears, Halo, Rainbow Six, sports, etc, and made sure they stayed true to their original vision

62049594
>>62049436
> Xbox is 100% dead

They deserve to die for what they did to Halo

62049604
>>62049571
Let's be honest, consoles, neither Xbox nor PlayStation, have not been a thing for like a decade. People just prefer PC.

62049618
>>62049604
Playstation and Switch are selling like crazy, they are making more console sales than ever. Xbox leadership is controlled by clueless idiots that makes millions a year to keep failing and surrounded by yes men and ass-kissers, I assume the Windows leadership is the same, does not bode well to have such a major failure as Xbox

62049635
>>62049571
gimme a full rundown of what happened

62049655
>>62049635
>gimme a full rundown of what happened

Basically they had the golden egg in Xbox 360, it was the biggest gaming craze ever since it catered to the college aged dude-bro market, everyone had an Xbox 360, it was close to perfect. They got cocky and for the next console after 360 they wanted to capture boomer market since they saw them buying the Nintendo Wii, this was the key moment they lost. Instead of doubling down on college-aged male market, they wanted to get boomers, girls, gays, etc. Xbox One reveal at E3 is famous since the entire segment was about attaching Cable TV/Television to your console, by this point Cable TV was dying so it was an insane decision.

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=LrvE3B5Rz6A

62049970
>>62047766
fml i just showed the entire internet how i find fvgs lol... have at it assholes. yes im high iq

62050160
>>62049557
ok, jared vennet from deutche bank lol

62050164
>>62049571
>xbox

holy shit your FA fucking sucks assuming that bullshit even works its a never ending guessing game dont even get me started nigga

sybau no amount of fa that isnt priced it will supersede my PA analysis skills stupid faggot

62050168
you're also forgetting iirc microsoft is one of the largest investors in Open AI lmao

62050173
A shoulder test like that rockets up or down. It's basically a coin flip but the velocity is such it'll snap your neck if you're wrong.
It's not even good for a degenerate gamble because volatility is already elevated past the 95th percentile, so your otm calls/puts can't moon.

62050175
>>62049970
>>62050160
>>62050164
>>62050168

t. jamboy

62050299
>MSAI shitting the bed
>W11 shitting the bed
>365 shitting the bed
>Xbox literally dead not even shitting the bed anymore
Great entry for a short position maybe.

62050512
>>62043947
Is anyone even using Windows for anything other than Claude?

Windows is becoming a Claude browser. You turn on your PC, open claude, and it does everything you used to use Windows for.

Then one day anthropic is going to implement a virtual machine and a file viewer directly into Claude, so you wont ever need to use Windows again.

Its over for microsoft at this point.

62050528
>>62043947
good luck with your jeetsoft

62050538
I boughted a $500 jan/2027 leap at $400
Watch these niggers go bankrupt

62050570
I work as a Dynamics365 software developer. Do you guys think is time to switch to another career?

62050633
>>62043947
Nobody has ever gone broke betting on Microsoft. They are a shit company, but the entire world runs on Excel spreadsheets.

62050873
>>62043947
Just do it. This is a temporary economic crisis almost at the same level as covid. You can set yourself up for a great situation once the dust settles. I was a millionaire after the crash in 2020 because I never stopped my DCA and I loaded up lumps sums of cash into tech stocks and BTC while everyone here was posting pinkjaks.

62050883
>>62043947
Hey, I work at Microsoft as a Senior Data Center Technician. It's an absolute mess. We're have soooo many data centers going up, including an OpenAI project, but with the cracks clearly showing in OpenAI's profitability, as well as Sora getting shut down, there is a lot of tension in the air. We just got a hiring freeze nationwide, if I'm not mistaken. Our network engineers are all outsourced to India. We do not have an on-site NOC at any of our facilities, as far as I'm aware. These street shitter network engineers are completely incompetent and projects drag because of them. In addition, dIvERsiTy iZ oUr StrEngt here, so a lot of new techs we hire are incompetent brown/black trash. One manager just hired his son. I'm probably going to be quitting the data center world once this bubble pops, which is most likely this year.

62038753

They dont really get in the way. Alot of these trannies have a small dick that doesn't get hard because of the estrogen. Its like a cute decoration that bounces around helplessly as you pound them in the ass.

62038821

It'd have to be for good-looking straight men and extremely feminine cute twinks only.

62042289

62042299

if you treat them like an actual 100% girl they will let you do anything to them. And you can nut in them all day with no pregnancy.

Buy land

You selling him? I might be interested...

62048262

no, mam. and I dont have the key to those toy handcuffs you got yourself into. find your way out of them, like youll find your way out of my hotel room. thanks.

62048265

item.title: If you had $150k in assets and $3k leftover every month from paychecks, would it make sense going all in index funds or… — 21 replies — 23.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
If you had $150k in assets and $3k leftover every month from paychecks, would it make sense going all in index funds or…

Open thread • Replies: 21 • Updated: 2026-03-30 06:06 UTC

OP

If you had $150k in assets and $3k leftover every month from paychecks, would it make sense going all in index funds or BTC or something else like building a business? Goal is to retire within a decade (I'm already 29)

Early replies (first 3)

62046106
>>62046068
78% of new businesses fail within a year, 93% fail within 5 years, and 97% fail within 10 years.
Just fyi.

62046111
>>62046106
So, then, what? Double down on my job + BTC/index funds and hope for the best?

62046115
>>62046111
pretty much yes minus the btc. dont do that.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62046119
>>62046115
just index funds? auto buy until I'm like 50s then? sucks to wait that long to retire. Right now I have 50k in short term treasury bills waiting to dca into btc. Do you think I should just buy VOO with it instead?

62046148
>>62046119
no one gets rich over night. its a slow ass grind but at least you have a very high chance of success. your time on this earth is limited and dont have unlimited attempts at shit like risky assets or business ideas.

62046166
>>62046111
>Double down on my job + index funds and hope for the best
Yes. Don't bother with BTC though.
>>62046119
>just index funds
Mostly index funds, yes. No more than about 10% in individual stocks. I've got a bit over $2m in the stock market, of that only $128k are in individual stocks, the rest are in index funds. While it's true that you can make bank investing in the right companies, "the right companies" are only obvious in hindsight. In the late 1980's the Next Big Thing was AI, but had you invested in Symbolics you would have lost everything within 5 years. In the late 1990's the Next Big Thing was the Internet, but had you invested in Sun Microsystems ("The network is the computer") you would have lost almost everything within 5 years.
>auto buy until I'm like 50s then
yes. It's very much a tortoise vs hare situation.

62046839
>>62046068
engaging data retirement calculator is useful to understand the power of investing solely in broad market index funds with an understood CAGR if you are ever tempted to stock pick and try to beat the market (that is, most people will not beat the market, and getting the market return means you will almost certainly retire within your timeline if you are comfortable with a moderate lifestyle).

Current research shows a 100% stock allocation is actually the correct decision for almost everyone, at any age (as opposed to bonds, precious metals, etc). From there it's ideal to have a good amount of international exposure while keeping a bias toward your home country - if you are in the US, say 60% US and 40% international. From Fama-French the data shows that a small-cap value tilt to the portfolio is also beneficial to capturing a risk premium, although it has not played out this way in recent decades and no one has a good explanation of why. Something like Avantis' funds AVDV and AVUV make it easy to target these tilts if you decide to go down that path.

See Scott Cederburg for actual analysis of all this, Ben Felix's podcast and his videos in general to make it digestible.

If you actually have something valuable for the world then of course a business will outperform, but the likelihood of that being true is very low when index investing can be done in such a way that at no point in history would you have failed - even considering things like retiring on Black Monday.

And during your working years, look at market downturns as a way to get more value for your 3k/month going in. Crashes are even better. Never pull out unless you're literally destitute.

62046887
>>62046068
Long btc 25x-30x it is safu.

62047357
>>62046068
all in btc, ignore the index retards.

62047358
Stack cash short term, we are only just edging onto the tip of the bad times.
Ignore BTC, it's had its day (It really is different this time).
Buy index funds in the medium to long term.

62047373
>>62046068
5% Bitcoin (in an etf or hot wallet).
5% Gold mining stocks.
85% Stocks.
5% Money Market Fund.

62047437
>>62046068
100% in physical silver

62047464
>>62046068
I was in your shoes when I was 29 and was able to retire last year at age 36 (actually could've done it a few years earlier). While working I moved 80%+ of my disposable income into BTC, ETH, and megacap tech stocks.
>picrelated

62047465
>>62047464
>recent finances for comparison

62047475
>>62047464
too bad you will be broke soon as trump destroys world economy

62047483
Going all in on WORLD market if not american. Stuff like XEQT if Canadian.
If you are american go all in on sp 500 is fine.

Don't bother with crypto or maybe 5 % in it.

Building a business if not fun for you or a hobby is painful and not a good return of the time you invested may as well do overtime or get a second job. Then again passive income very good.

62047489
>>62047465
>crypto
>750k
saggy baggies

62050791
all in btc

62050906
>>62046119
that's the game for people like you and me. There is a 3% chance you make it and retire before 35 and live a life of luxury. But you can only make that gamble once, and if you fuck you'll wage for the rest of your life and never retire. Trust me, you want to play it safe. The bigger that number gets, the faster it gets bigger-er.
>Time in the market beats timing the market
It's true. It's always been true, don't fight against it.

62050981
>>62046068
why do only whites and browns want to retire? jews to ever retire early

62050999
>>62050981
Whites are in hard wagie jobs. Construction, jobs where you're on your feet, IT, programming. Hard jobs that are demanding and take 8+ hours 5 days a week.
Jews get themselves into extremely easy high paying high status jobs. Lawyer. Judge. Various board level positions. Specialty doctor. Dentist. Jobs where they work 4 hours a day a few days a week, at most.
My wife is a doctor and teaches med school students sometimes and I don't think she has ever worked an 8 hour day. Ever. She leaves at like 8 am and is home by 2 pm. She plans to work until she's 80.

item.title: ½ Trillion Gone — 74 replies — 17.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
½ Trillion Gone

Open thread • Replies: 74 • Updated: 2026-03-30 02:38 UTC

OP

The US stock market wiped out $500 Billion dollars today. You did sell all your goyboxes, didn't you anon?

Early replies (first 3)

62036869
Haha, just kidding. It was actually a $1 Trillion dollars.

62036893
i bought the dip
panicans lose
TTP

62036894
unless you're a day trader none of this matters and even if you're a day trader still none of this matters you will lose your money regardless. fud doesn't work on proper investment strategies. longing the market won game over bye bye pink wojaks farming crypto queer

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62037501
perhaps today was not the best day to try to get into trading methinks

62037510
You can always tell the /pol/faggots apart because they think that even the worst market downturn in history is some kind of spiral that will never end. They're unironically the same kind of faggots that get hyped up when a hurricane comes in and keep spamming about it when it's already gone hoping the death toll will rise. Total fucking losers lol

62037531
Whoever made this trade is probably dead now with a bullet in the head.

62037566
>>62036853
>DJIA dropped 1%
>S&P 500 dropped 1.7%
>Nasdaq-100 dropped 2.4%
OMG it's a fucking nothingburger! However can the West survive an event of this insignificance!

62037573
>>62037465
>It's concerning from a tax receipts perspective
Only if you're Dutch. Americans don't have a tax on unrealized capital gains.

62037605
>>62037510
Japanese women are so pleased with themselves!
They saved the japanese stock market with their female japanese asian women tendencies and birthrates and JAV appearances.
The yen was saved.
The japanese race is saved!

62037625
>>62037470
Its actually kind of crazy how many people don't realize this and keep acting like we should try to bring our wars in under budget. The money is infinite. It always has been infinite, it always will be infinite. There is enough money to drop a six million dollar missile on top of every individual brown person on the planet, it makes absolutely no difference.

62037699
>>62036969
Even though that chart is calculated “without inflation” it’s still affected by it.
In that there’s still a gravitational pull from scared fiat looking to park itself. So participation still increases in absolute terms.

62037786
>>62036853
I voted for this

62037819
>>62037501
It’s unironically a great day. Investing is the opposite of traffic lights. Buy red. Hold green. Sell calls and puts for premiums when it crabs. It’s really that simple. Theres going to be a lot of money made on shipping, weapons, and oil for anyone with a brain. If the market trends down invest in coffee. Its a really undersold defensive because white bitches no matter how broke will never give up their Starslops.

62038287
>>62037625
Your fucking retarded, America has crumbling infrastructure, no real manufacturing base, gutted educational standards, no major sources of economic growth accounting for inflation and a decimated middle.

The 8+ trillion plus dollars and other resources we spent on the middle east forever wars, could have easily propelled America into a global economic behemoth that dwarfs China and the rest of planet if it was well invested.

62038473
>>62036908
LMAO

62038644
>>62038287
But muh national security

62038720
>>62037293
we are also living in an era where anyone with a smartphone can be there own broker. kids growing up today are investing into markets which will keep the trend line up more than ever before. compound these facts with rampant consumerism. it's not a good time to be a bear generally speaking

62042487
>>62037625
Do you perhaps work for the Treasury Dept under the Trump administration?
You sound just retarded enough.

62042521
>>62036853
Insanely bullish for gold.

62042954
>>62036869
WHAT THE FUCK 1,000,0000,00000 TRILLONS YOU MEAN? HOW THE FUCK THE FED PRINTED SO MANY FAKE MONEY?? LMFAO

62043042
>>62036869
woooooooow so many zeroes

62043961
>>62036853
I voted for this

62045345
>>62037625
money is a measurement of value, and you can't print value. all this spending and inflation has yet come home to roost

62046233
>>62043042
how much is 1 trillion trillion

62046276
>>62037465
it's funny how ukrainians have such an inflated ego. They kidnap people from the streets to send them to war and they think they can teach america about war? kek

62046618
>>62037465
Our military spending is nothing.
Only 15 cents on every dollar you pay in taxes goes to it.
15 more cents goes to paying interest because our boomer overlords couldn’t balance the budget.
70 cents literally just goes to boomers, through SS, medicaid, etc. (Though there are a few fraudulent outliers like those Somalian daycares.)

Meanwhile in china
Percentages of government spending.
Social Security & Employment: ~15.4%
Education: ~15.1%
Agriculture, Forestry & Water: ~8.2%
Health and Family Planning: ~7.4%
Urban & Rural Community Affairs: ~7.2%
General Public Service: ~7.2%
Science & Technology: ~4.2%
Transportation: ~4.2%
National Defense: ~5.9% (Probably under reported tho)
Interest Payments on Debt: ~4.7%

The fucking Chicom boomers could run our country better than the neoliberal and neoconservative boomers have.

62046627
>>62046618
I will counterpoint that a lot of the stuff on the chinese budget is just jobs programs bullshit make-work.
But it’s still somehow better than the current US setup of 70% (!) retirement home money and the rest is just MIC grift and interest.

62046644
>>62037625
>Just print more money

62046806
>>62037100
When Iran decides its over. They control the Hormuz straight (and soon, the Red Sea), America can't do shit about it, and they aren't in the mood to negotiate because A: They're winning and B: Orange nigger and his mossad buddies use negotiations as an opportunity for assassination perfidy

62047109
>>62036969
you can see where raegan 5x'd the national debt

62047195
>>62046618
You're not seeing the full picture. 60% of industry in China is state-owned, hence industrial spending is represented in government expenditure. But it's the same in United States - the vast majority of "state spending" occurs via the issuance and rehypothecation of public and private credit, enabled by the central bank, which is only nominally independent. The US larps as a free economy when actively it is controlled by banks and corporate interests that tell you that social security is the problem and not the trillions going into their pockets.

62047266
>>62036853
stocks are a bubble and a scam, you don't own anything if you have stocks and you don't finance anything in the economy

62050467
>>62047195
That’s a fair point but banking exists in both systems and they’re different to what i‘m talking about.
The banks, even the state run ones in china, operate on the basis that the money you put in will at least still be there the day after tomorrow.
Taxes have no such basis, you pay them and they’re gone, they aren’t coming back.

But China’s government (tax budget) spending is still technically ~30% of GDP like the US’s.
If the governments are taking the same amount anyways, I would take the chink bridges-to-nowhere tax budget ANYYY day over the USA’s 70% spend on old people, and ““disability”” grifters.

I was expecting something around $40k.

and to think that there are "people" in this board that religiously watch every fucking video of his , hes worse than capo.

came to say this

Stocks will be sideways at best this year Gold has no reason for buying power to step in again Your best bet this year i…

Open thread • Replies: 53 • Updated: 2026-03-29 22:42 UTC

OP

Gold has no reason for buying power to step in again

Your best bet this year is BTC. Prove me wrong

Early replies (first 3)

62038234
>>62038225
Stop posting CP
Also I’ve lost so much money recently it’s going to take me hopefully 5yrs again to break even.

62038243
If your best bet is BTC, then MSTR is the best bet as it's an engine that accretes more BTC continuously.

62038259
ive never seen such a young man

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62039156
>>62038225
>Your best bet this year is BTC. Prove me wrong
There are a bunch of better plays that will pump harder next year.

62039163
>>62038333
The amount of seething from zoomers is unbelievable, I live near a college campus and regularly see bald or wrinkled people walking around in clothes for teens and it takes me a second to figure out that they're not 50yos trying to relive their youth, but incredibly aged and fucked up looking 18yos.

62039725
>>62038355
That dude has horrible genetics and looks his age. I actually love cocky zoomers like you, specially the ones that are already showing signs of baldness and nasolabia wrinkles in their early to mid 20s. My father is pushing 70 and looks like hes in his late 40s so i know how i will age over time. Sorry you didnt win the genetic lottery, keep on malding zoom zoom.

62039736
>>62039163
Yeah i think they are traumatized with how badly they are aging nowadays. Some zoomers in their early 20s look like they are in their 30s. I would also be coping like these retards if i was in the same boat ngl must be brutal
>Eheh at least age number says im young!
Yeah cope harder zoomtards lmao

62039737
>>62039725
Is that all it is? My Dad looks great too but he still gets up at 3:39 to go to the gym in the morning. He's almost 70.

I feel part of me retaining my looks is all the exercise and healthy eating he made me do that zooms def aren't getting.

62039764
>>62038593
You don't really lose money if the cash is worth more when you sell. But you lose money from inflation with the cash in the bank. This is how I see the PM investments. Keep some money in a bank account for emergencies and deflation. PMs for inflation and rare gold bull runs every few decades.

62039767
>>62039737
Its not all. The most important factors are genetics, stress level, sleep quality, diet and sunlight exposure. Intense exercise ages you, you should do moderate exercise only. The people that lived the longest did moderate exercise daily throughtout their entire life (daily tasks stuff that make your body move and be active)

62039774
Another nice ultrasonography, can't wait for him to be born

62040216
>>62038274
you will never age gracefully with that attitude

62040227
>>62038274
>seething at his looks

62040284
>>62038333
That's why it is memed. It was necessary.

62040416
>>62038225
I don't take investment advice from 17 yr old boys anon. Go frolic outside kiddo, leave the market to us grownups.

62040501
>>62038274
Hag cope

62040509
>>62038333
The boy is literally me.
Im 31 and I just had someone tell me I look 23

62040512
>>62038225

62040544
>>62039725
your kids must feel so lucky lol

62042262
>>62040509
im 40 and a girl said i look 24, we are all gonna make it

62042471
>>62038225
It is okay, if that's your selfie you are 16 at most, so you have time to invest

62043286
>>62038274
All good Memes are good because some people get triggered by them

62043287
>>62038333
>>62040284
Yes exactly. And yes im one of them and people call me 5 years younger often. But most people think they look younger.

62046138
>>62043287
In fact a girl who is 30 and looks 30 just told me people "think" aka tell her she looks 5 years younger.

62046388
>>62038274
old hands typed this

62046408
>>62038333
I mean, I am a millennial that's mistaken for being younger, but for 25ish instead of being a high schooler. I never knew that was a stereotype until the meme came out.

62046524
>>62038225
Sincerely, i wish this young man the best.

62046541
>>62038225
I'd trade all my BTC just to be as young as this boy again

62046555
cute lil fella, looks like he's gonna grow up to be a strong man

62046790
This guy died btw.

62046890
Whats his secret? He looks like he hasnt aged a single day

62049431
yep

62049442
>>62038225
MILLIONAIRE HERE. theres literally ntohing to buy. only wait for the crash

losers who I NEED MONEY NOW deserve to stay forever poor. the amount of so called "rich people" who live this existence are pathetic and must be purged.

item.title: BTC Black Swan Event — 60 replies — 6.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
BTC Black Swan Event

Open thread • Replies: 60 • Updated: 2026-03-29 21:17 UTC

OP

THIS IS IT!!!!
HERE WE GOOOOOOOOOOOO

Early replies (first 3)

62039162
What the fuck is happening...

62039175
>>62039162
crabbing

62039176
Posting in epic bread

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62040818
>>62040745
im sure its safer in your stick house
when the shit really hits the fan, no one will trade your shiny rock for cans of food. assuming it doesnt get stolen by armed joggers first.

62040830
>>62040700
it has to keep its value to be a store of value

62040895
>>62040521
I thought it was Marathon dumping everything they had to desperately pay off debt?

62041034
>>62040830
bitcoin is doing almost as bad in the same time frame...
and infinitely worse if you zoom out up to like 5 years...
besides your use case wasn't about store of value it was about evading governments. XRP is a lot better than BTC for that.

62041050
>>62040054
m8 i dont give a crap if its real or not. It makes me easy money, if handle correctly

62041059
>>62041050
>>62040054
Im OP btw. Somehow my id change even though i use the same wifi

62041071
>>62039143
Overture, curtains, lights
This is it, tonight's the night

62041093
>>62039143
You retards really don't know what a black swan even is lmao
It has already came and went.
>>62039223
>The price went down in a completely different way at a completely different spot
>IT'S THE SAME PUTTURN GOIS!!
This guy can't even into TA

62041217
>>62041093
How do you define a low? Because we've broken below the low of March 9th.

62041609
>>62041217
That's not even a low. That's just random micro noise, pure chop.
Lows are defined as the low of an impulse move.

The move from feb 24th to march 16th is one move with one low.

There is for certain one low, ~60k. The second low is extremely debatable, and might not be a valid low. But, probably is because it creates a new high in the range.
But, without a shadow of a doubt, 66 and 67k are NOT lows.

62041745
>>62039143
>""Black Swan""
Yeah, that's not what's happening.
>Sell off was telegraphed a mile away with options expiring and MARA being desperate for cash
>Dumb fucks seriously thought 75k was a realistic max pain threshold in a bear market, they get what's coming to them
>Everyone already prepped with buy orders at 65k
>Recovery back to 71k expected by monday at the soonest if all continues as is and Trump stays on the golf course and out of the war room

62041768
>>62041609
they are lows compared to yesterday

62041794
>>62041745
I bought in at 68. How am I doing? Let's just say it would be the difference between 1.09 and 1.14 Bitcoin

62041922
>>62041768
That's a completely meaningless sentence. Lows are structural. Intraday lows are just noise.
Perspectives like yours are exactly why people don't believe in TA. Because they cannot accurately define what a low is. They'll see random choppy noise and call it lower lows, then the price will flip to the upside and break a high and they'll say TA is useless.


Calling every little bit of chop a low or a high is just retarded. If you cannot accurately define what highs and lows are, you're doomed to be chopped up and stopped out.

62041939
>>62039143
THIS IS IT
*bangs fingertip on table*
THIS IS IT

62041976
All you have to do is look at the weekly to see these are completely different patterns.

62041994
>>62041794
My opinion? Hold for now until shit stabalises. Set you next buy order at 67.2k if you want to be cautious and avoid buying blood. Next cycle will probably peak at 71k, maybe 72k if the market starts feeling bullish but there's no way we're going to see any massive bullruns anytime soon.

62042239
>>62039143
Sundump imminent.

62042587
>>62042239
Highly unlikely for the simple reason that there's nothing left to dump.

62042791
>>62041745
>>Recovery back to 71k expected by monday at the soonest

Why is this expected? Ask any ai and they say it's unlikely.

>>62041994
Of course I'm going to hold it. I'm not going to lose several thousand by being impatient.

62042808
>>62041994
When Jews get what they want, Bitcoin goes up. When they don't get what they want, Bitcoin goes down. Looks like they'll be getting 10,000 American troops this weekend so we'll find out if my metric is reliable

62042836

And there's your problem. Do your own analysis, and don't listen to any AI. All of them tell you to be a good goy and steer clear of trading entirely.
The 71k figure is based on the wall of 65k buy orders ready to spring the price back, and past patterns showing that the previous stability point of the last cycle usually ends up being the peak of the next. It's widely theorised this is going to continue till at least October/November where it will probably be around the 50k mark.
This is all dependant on Trump doing absolutely fucking nothing for the next 48 hours though. The weekend has to be quiet, or punters are going to panic sell, as if USD is somehow going to save them.
